In the Name of Allah, the Most Beneficent, the Most Merciful Introduction

All praise and thanks are to Allah, we praise Him and we seek His Aid and we ask His Forgiveness; and we seek refuge with Allah from the evil of ourselves and the wickedness of our deeds. Whomsoever Allah guides, there is none to misguide him and whomsoever He leaves astray, there is none who can guide him. And I testify that none is worthy of worship except Allah and I testify that Muhammad is His servant and His Messenger, may the peace of Allah and bountiful blessings be upon him and upon his family and Companions. As for what follows:

Some brothers, may Allah reward them with goodness, have expressed a desire to see some of the questions related to the pillars of Islam which were asked of our Shaikh, the most learned Shaikh, Muhammad bin Salih Al-‘Uthaimeen may Allah, the Most High bless him. The hope was for them to be published in the Arabic language and following that, to be translated into many different languages in order to spread the benefit of them. Indeed, these verdicts are distinguished by their reliance upon the Book of Allah, the Most High, the Sunnah of His Messenger ## and the opinions of the scholars who are known for their investigative powers. So I put the matter to our Shaikh may Allah bless him and reward him for his support for Islam and the Muslims with the best of rewards and he welcomed the idea and encouraged it, since it was a form of mutual cooperation in encouraging the good and forbidding the evil, and of spreading Islamic knowledge.

” After His Eminence expressed his agreement and gave his advice, the process of gathering these Fatawa and selecting them from the whole collection of the Fatawa began and continued until the work was completed.

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 16 PHY Osh subs

I ask Allah, the Almighty, the All-Powerful by His Grace and Generosity to make this work beneficial and to make it purely for His Noble Face, and that He reward His Eminence, our Shaikh with the best of rewards, and to bless him in his knowledge, his deeds and his life-span. Verily, Allah is Beneficent and Generous. May the peace and blessings of Allah be upon our Prophet, Muhammad and upon all of his family and his Companions.

Written by: Fahd bin Nasir As-Sulaiman.

Fatawa on ‘Agidah (Creed) 19 Badal! oo ght

Fatawa on ‘Aqgidah (Creed)

Q. 1. What is the definition of Tawhid and what are its types?

A. Linguistically, Tawhid is the infinitive of the verb Wahhada (past tense), Yuwahhidu (present tense), i.e. to make something one; and this cannot be achieved except by negation and confirmation: The negation of that rule for anything except that which is declared to be one and confirmation of it for that thing.

So we say for example: Tawhid is not achieved by a person until he testifies that none is worthy of worship except Allah and he rejects the right of any other besides Allah, Almighty, AllPowerful to be worshipped and he confirms it for Allah, Alone. This is because mere negation means complete denial while absolute confirmation does not prevent another being included in that designation. So if you said for example: So-and-so is standing, you have in this case confirmed that he is standing, but you have not declared him to be the only one standing, as if you said: There is no one standing except Zaid, in that case, you would have declared Zaid to be the only one standing, since you had negated anyone besides him standing. This is the achievement of true Tawhid, that is to say that Tawhid is not Tawhid unless it includes negation and confirmation.

The types of Tawhid regarding Allah, the Almighty, the AllPowerful are all included in a general definition, which is: The Oneness of Allah, the Most Glorified, the Most High in what is unique to Him. And according to what the scholars have said, they are three:

‘1. Tawhid Ar-Rububiyyah (The Oneness of Allah in matters of Lordship).

2. Tawhid Al-Uluhiyyah (The Oneness of Allah in matters related to divinity).

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 20 PLY ots yf Gols

3. Tawhid Al-Asma’ was-Sifat (The Oneness of Allah in His Names and Divine Attributes).

They have come to know this by investigation, research, and by examining the (Qur’anic) Verses and Ahadith; and they have found that Tawhid does not fall outside these three types, and so they have classified Zawhid into three categories:

1, Tawhid Ar-Rububiyyah, and that is: Affirming the Oneness of Allah, the Most Glorified, the Most High in creation, dominion and organizing (the affairs of the universe) and the detailed explanation of that is:

Firstly: Regarding the affirmation of the Oneness of Allah, the Most High in creation, it is Allah Alone Who is the Creator, and there is no creator besides Him; Allah, the Most High says:

And Allah, the Most High says, making clear the falseness of the pagan’s gods:

i wow 7 Poe ae . ip

4G YSN at gs Si HA EG “Is there any creator other than Allah who provides for you from

the sky (rain) and the earth? La ilaha illa Huwa (none has the

right to be worshipped but He)."") So Allah, the Most High, Alone is the Creator and He decreed all things and arranged them in due proportion. His creation includes all that result from what He has done as well as what His creatures do. This is why it is a part of complete belief in Qadar (the Divine Decree) to believe that Allah, the Most High is the Creator of the deeds of His ee as Allah, the Most High says:

Cf25 SG xe BEY od JE BE

“Is then He, Who creates as one who creates Will you not then remember?)

Fatawa on ‘Agidah (Creed) 21 Baa! og ghd

This means that creature’s deed is one of his attributes, and the worshipper is created by Allah, and the creator of a thing is also the creator of its attributes. Another aspect of this is that the deed of the worshipper takes place by an absolutely certain will and complete ability; and both the will and the ability are creations of Allah, the Almighty, the All-Powerful, and the Creator of the complete cause is the Creator of the effect.

Dengan Nama Allah, Yang Maha Pemurah, Yang Maha Penyayang Pendahuluan

Segala puji dan syukur bagi Allah, kami memuji-Nya dan kami memohon pertolongan-Nya serta meminta ampunan-Nya; dan kami berlindung kepada Allah dari kejahatan diri kami sendiri dan keburukan amal perbuatan kami. Barangsiapa yang Allah beri petunjuk, tidak ada yang dapat menyesatkannya dan barangsiapa yang Ia biarkan sesat, tidak ada yang dapat memberinya petunjuk. Dan aku bersaksi bahwa tidak ada yang berhak diibadahi kecuali Allah dan aku bersaksi bahwa Muhammad adalah hamba dan utusan-Nya, semoga keselamatan Allah dan keberkahan yang berlimpah terlimpahkan kepadanya, kepada keluarganya dan para Sahabatnya. Adapun yang berikut ini:

Beberapa saudara, semoga Allah membalas mereka dengan kebaikan, telah menyatakan keinginan untuk melihat sebagian pertanyaan yang berkaitan dengan rukun-rukun Islam yang pernah ditanyakan kepada Syaikh kami, Syaikh yang sangat terpelajar, Muhammad bin Salih Al-'Utsaimin semoga Allah Yang Maha Tinggi memberkatinya. Harapannya adalah agar hal-hal tersebut diterbitkan dalam bahasa Arab dan setelah itu diterjemahkan ke dalam berbagai bahasa agar manfaatnya tersebar luas. Sesungguhnya, fatawa-fatawa ini memiliki keistimewaan karena berlandaskan pada Kitab Allah Yang Maha Tinggi, Sunnah Rasul-Nya ## dan pendapat para ulama yang dikenal dengan kemampuan investigasi mereka. Maka aku menyampaikan hal ini kepada Syaikh kami semoga Allah memberkatinya dan membalasnya atas dukungannya terhadap Islam dan kaum Muslimin dengan sebaik-baik balasan, dan beliau menyambut gagasan tersebut serta mendorong pelaksanaannya, karena hal itu merupakan bentuk kerja sama dalam menganjurkan kebaikan dan mencegah kemungkaran, serta dalam menyebarkan ilmu Islam.

" Setelah Yang Mulia menyatakan persetujuannya dan memberikan sarannya, proses pengumpulan fatawa-fatawa ini dan pemilihannya dari seluruh kumpulan fatawa dimulai dan terus berlanjut hingga pekerjaan tersebut selesai.

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 16 PHY Osh subs

Aku memohon kepada Allah Yang Maha Agung, Yang Maha Kuasa dengan rahmat dan kedermawanan-Nya agar menjadikan karya ini bermanfaat dan menjadikannya murni untuk Wajah-Nya Yang Mulia, serta agar Ia membalas Yang Mulia, Syaikh kami dengan sebaik-baik balasan, dan memberkati beliau dalam ilmu, amal, dan usia panjangnya. Sesungguhnya, Allah Maha Pemurah dan Maha Dermawan. Semoga keselamatan dan keberkahan Allah terlimpahkan kepada Nabi kami, Muhammad dan kepada seluruh keluarga serta para Sahabatnya.

Ditulis oleh: Fahd bin Nasir As-Sulaiman.

Fatawa tentang 'Aqidah (Keyakinan) 19 Badal! oo ght

Fatawa tentang 'Aqidah (Keyakinan)

Q. 1. Apa definisi tawhid dan apa saja jenisnya?

A. Secara bahasa, tawhid adalah masdar dari kata kerja Wahhada (bentuk lampau), Yuwahhidu (bentuk sekarang), yakni menjadikan sesuatu itu satu; dan hal ini tidak dapat tercapai kecuali dengan negasi dan konfirmasi: Negasi aturan tersebut dari segala sesuatu selain yang dinyatakan sebagai satu dan konfirmasinya untuk hal tersebut.

Maka kita katakan misalnya: Tawhid tidak tercapai oleh seseorang hingga ia bersaksi bahwa tidak ada yang berhak diibadahi kecuali Allah dan ia menolak hak siapapun selain Allah Yang Maha Agung, Maha Kuasa untuk diibadahi serta mengukuhkannya hanya untuk Allah semata. Hal ini karena negasi semata berarti penolakan total sementara konfirmasi mutlak tidak menghalangi keikutsertaan pihak lain dalam sebutan tersebut. Sehingga jika Anda berkata misalnya: Si Fulan sedang berdiri, dalam hal ini Anda telah mengkonfirmasi bahwa ia sedang berdiri, tetapi Anda belum menyatakannya sebagai satu-satunya yang berdiri, berbeda jika Anda berkata: Tidak ada yang berdiri kecuali Zaid, dalam hal itu, Anda telah menyatakan Zaid sebagai satu-satunya yang berdiri, karena Anda telah menafikan selainnya untuk berdiri. Inilah pencapaian tawhid yang sejati, yakni tawhid tidak menjadi tawhid kecuali mencakup negasi dan konfirmasi.

Jenis-jenis tawhid mengenai Allah Yang Maha Agung, Maha Kuasa semuanya tercakup dalam definisi umum, yaitu: Keesaan Allah Yang Maha Mulia, Maha Tinggi dalam hal-hal yang khusus bagi-Nya. Dan menurut apa yang dikatakan para ulama, jenisnya ada tiga:

'1. Tawhid Ar-Rububiyyah (Keesaan Allah dalam masalah Ketuhanan).

2. Tawhid Al-Uluhiyyah (Keesaan Allah dalam masalah yang berkaitan dengan ketuhanan).

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 20 PLY ots yf Gols

3. Tawhid Al-Asma' was-Sifat (Keesaan Allah dalam Nama-nama dan Sifat-sifat Ilahi-Nya).

Para ulama mengetahui hal ini melalui penelitian, kajian, dan dengan menelaah ayat-ayat (Al-Qur'an) dan hadith; dan mereka menemukan bahwa tawhid tidak keluar dari tiga jenis ini, sehingga mereka mengklasifikasikan tawhid ke dalam tiga kategori:

1, Tawhid Ar-Rububiyyah, yaitu: Menegaskan keesaan Allah, Yang Maha Mulia, Yang Maha Tinggi dalam penciptaan, kekuasaan, dan pengaturan (urusan alam semesta), dan penjelasan rincinya adalah:

Pertama: Mengenai penegasan keesaan Allah, Yang Maha Tinggi dalam penciptaan, hanya Allah-lah satu-satunya Pencipta, dan tidak ada pencipta selain-Nya; Allah, Yang Maha Tinggi berfirman:

Dan Allah, Yang Maha Tinggi berfirman, menjelaskan kebatilan tuhan-tuhan orang-orang musyrik:

i wow 7 Poe ae . ip

4G YSN at gs Si HA EG "Adakah pencipta selain Allah yang memberi rezeki kepada kalian dari

langit (hujan) dan bumi? La ilaha illa Huwa (tiada yang berhak

diibadahi selain Dia)."") Maka Allah, Yang Maha Tinggi, sendiri-lah Sang Pencipta dan Dia menetapkan segala sesuatu serta mengaturnya dengan ukuran yang tepat. Ciptaan-Nya mencakup semua yang dihasilkan dari apa yang Dia lakukan maupun apa yang dilakukan oleh makhluk-makhluk-Nya. Inilah sebabnya menjadi bagian dari iman yang sempurna terhadap Qadar (Takdir Ilahi) untuk meyakini bahwa Allah, Yang Maha Tinggi adalah Pencipta perbuatan hamba-hamba-Nya, sebagaimana Allah, Yang Maha Tinggi berfirman:

Cf25 SG xe BEY od JE BE

"Maka apakah yang menciptakan itu sama dengan yang tidak menciptakan? Tidakkah kamu mengambil pelajaran?"

Fatawa tentang 'Aqidah 21 Baa! og ghd

Ini berarti bahwa perbuatan makhluk adalah salah satu sifatnya, dan hamba diciptakan oleh Allah, dan pencipta sesuatu adalah pula pencipta sifat-sifatnya. Aspek lain dari ini adalah bahwa perbuatan hamba terjadi melalui kehendak yang pasti dan kemampuan yang sempurna; dan baik kehendak maupun kemampuan tersebut adalah ciptaan Allah, Yang Maha Perkasa, Yang Maha Kuasa, dan Pencipta sebab yang sempurna adalah Pencipta akibatnya.

In the Name of الله, the Most Beneficent, the Most Merciful Introduction All praise and thanks are to الله, we praise Him and we seek His Aid and we ask His Forgiveness; and we seek refuge with الله from the evil of ourselves and the wickedness of our deeds. Whomsoever الله guides, there is none to misguide him and whomsoever He leaves astray, there is none who can guide him. And I testify that none is worthy of worship except الله and I testify that Muhammad is His servant and His Messenger, may the peace of الله and bountiful blessings be upon him and upon his family and الصحابة. As for what follows: Some brothers, may الله reward them with goodness, have expressed a desire to see some of the questions related to the pillars of الإسلام which were asked of our Shaikh, the most learned Shaikh, Muhammad bin Salih Al-‘Uthaimeen may الله, the Most High bless him. The hope was for them to be published in the Arabic language and following that, to be translated into many different languages in order to spread the benefit of them. Indeed, these verdicts are distinguished by their reliance upon the Book of الله, the Most High, السنة النبوية of His Messenger ## and the opinions of the العلماء who are known for their investigative powers. So I put the matter to our Shaikh may الله bless him and reward him for his support for الإسلام and the المسلمون with the best of rewards and he welcomed the idea and encouraged it, since it was a form of mutual cooperation in encouraging the good and forbidding the evil, and of spreading الإسلامي knowledge. ” After His Eminence expressed his agreement and gave his advice, the process of gathering these الفتاوى and selecting them from the whole collection of the الفتاوى began and continued until the work was completed. الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام 16 PHY Osh subs I ask الله, the Almighty, the All-Powerful by His Grace and Generosity to make this work beneficial and to make it purely for His Noble Face, and that He reward His Eminence, our Shaikh with the best of rewards, and to bless him in his knowledge, his deeds and his life-span. Verily, الله is Beneficent and Generous. May the peace and blessings of الله be upon our Prophet, Muhammad and upon all of his family and his الصحابة. Written by: Fahd bin Nasir As-Sulaiman. الفتاوى on ‘Agidah (Creed) 19 Badal! oo ght الفتاوى on ‘Aqgidah (Creed) Q. 1. What is the definition of التوحيد and what are its types? A. Linguistically, التوحيد is the infinitive of the verb Wahhada (past tense), Yuwahhidu (present tense), i.e. to make something one; and this cannot be achieved except by negation and confirmation: The negation of that rule for anything except that which is declared to be one and confirmation of it for that thing. So we say for example: التوحيد is not achieved by a person until he testifies that none is worthy of worship except الله and he rejects the right of any other besides الله, Almighty, AllPowerful to be worshipped and he confirms it for الله, Alone. This is because mere negation means complete denial while absolute confirmation does not prevent another being included in that designation. So if you said for example: So-and-so is standing, you have in this case confirmed that he is standing, but you have not declared him to be the only one standing, as if you said: There is no one standing except Zaid, in that case, you would have declared Zaid to be the only one standing, since you had negated anyone besides him standing. This is the achievement of true التوحيد, that is to say that التوحيد is not التوحيد unless it includes negation and confirmation. The types of التوحيد regarding الله, the Almighty, the AllPowerful are all included in a general definition, which is: The Oneness of الله, the Most Glorified, the Most High in what is unique to Him. And according to what the العلماء have said, they are three: ‘1. التوحيد Ar-Rububiyyah (The Oneness of الله in matters of Lordship). 2. التوحيد Al-Uluhiyyah (The Oneness of الله in matters related to divinity). الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام 20 PLY ots yf Gols 3. التوحيد Al-Asma’ was-Sifat (The Oneness of الله in His Names and Divine Attributes). They have come to know this by investigation, research, and by examining the (Qur’anic) Verses and Ahadith; and they have found that التوحيد does not fall outside these three types, and so they have classified Zawhid into three categories: 1, التوحيد Ar-Rububiyyah, and that is: Affirming the Oneness of الله, the Most Glorified, the Most High in creation, dominion and organizing (the affairs of the universe) and the detailed explanation of that is: Firstly: Regarding the affirmation of the Oneness of الله, the Most High in creation, it is الله Alone Who is the Creator, and there is no creator besides Him; الله, the Most High says: And الله, the Most High says, making clear the falseness of the pagan’s gods: i wow 7 Poe ae . ip 4G YSN at gs Si HA EG “Is there any creator other than الله who provides for you from the sky (rain) and the earth? La ilaha illa Huwa (none has the right to be worshipped but He)."") So الله, the Most High, Alone is the Creator and He decreed all things and arranged them in due proportion. His creation includes all that result from what He has done as well as what His creatures do. This is why it is a part of complete belief in Qadar (the Divine Decree) to believe that الله, the Most High is the Creator of the deeds of His ee as الله, the Most High says: Cf25 SG xe BEY od JE BE “Is then He, Who creates as one who creates Will you not then remember?) الفتاوى on ‘Agidah (Creed) 21 Baa! og ghd This means that creature’s deed is one of his attributes, and the worshipper is created by الله, and the creator of a thing is also the creator of its attributes. Another aspect of this is that the deed of the worshipper takes place by an absolutely certain الوصية and complete ability; and both the الوصية and the ability are creations of الله, the Almighty, the All-Powerful, and the Creator of the complete cause is the Creator of the effect.
